what_it_does: Renders a small chip control that fetches the set of available embedder layers from the API, and lets the user pick which embedding layer ('lens') to read meaning through. It hides itself when only one layer exists, maps the 'default' layer to a null embedding value, and opens an animated menu of choices.
observations: Fetches layers via fetchLayers() on mount and computes a sorted union of layer names across all spaces
Treats 'default' as the base layer and converts it to emb=null when selected
Uses a custom useChipMenu hook for open/close/toggle and menu ref behavior
Uses framer-motion AnimatePresence for the dropdown entrance/exit
Applies stamp() data attributes for UI telemetry on the chip, current button, and each layer option
Silently swallows fetch errors so a missing endpoint just hides the chip
Encodes the layer name into the stamp path with encodeURIComponent
standards: Registry-true layer list: no hardcoded embedder set, new layers appear automatically
Hides the control entirely when there is no real choice
Maps the visible 'default' label back to null state
conventions: Custom hook encapsulates menu interaction state
Imports motion transition from a tokens file
Uses stamp() for declarative UI instrumentation
Uses aria-expanded on the toggle button
concerns: fetchLayers errors are caught and ignored, so failures are invisible
No loading or error state while layers are being fetched
Layer list is recomputed on every mount with no caching
Union across all spaces may show layers irrelevant to the current space
menuClass comes from useChipMenu but its shape is not visible in this file
notes: A self-configuring embedder-layer picker that stays out of the way when there is only one way to read meaning.
questions: What does useChipMenu return exactly, and what values can menuClass take?
What is the shape of fetchLayers()'s response and how are spaces keyed?
Is the union across spaces intentional, or should the menu show only layers for the current space?
What does stamp() do with these ui://controls/layer paths?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a registry-driven picker for choosing which embedding layer to use, with default mapped to null and graceful invisibility when no choice exists.
apparent_themes: Semantic lens / multiple ways to read meaning
intention_signals: The file is trying to give users a lightweight way to switch between different embedding 'readers' of the same data, while keeping the surface code free of hardcoded layer lists and silently removing itself when the choice is irrelevant.
novelty: An embedder-layer selector that discovers its own options from the engine's self-description rather than a static menu, making new embedding layers automatically available in the UI.
contribution: Provides a dynamic, self-populating layer selector that exposes available embedding readers, maps the base layer cleanly to null, and removes itself when there is nothing to choose, keeping the surrounding surface simple.
summary_for_embedding: LayerChip is a React component that fetches available embedder layers from an API, renders a chip-style selector when more than one layer exists, and sets the active embedding layer while mapping 'default' to null.
intention_for_embedding: The component was built to let users choose which embedding model/layer interprets the data, using a registry-true list that requires no surface edits when new layers are added.

symbols.does: Renders a registry-driven chip for selecting the active embedder layer and hides itself when only one layer is available.
symbols.description: LayerChip is a React component that fetches the union of embedder layers from /api/layers, keeps them in local state, and presents a small button showing the current layer. When opened, it displays an animated menu of all layers; selecting 'default' sets the embedding value to null, while selecting any other layer passes that name to setEmb. It returns null when there is only one layer, and silently ignores fetch failures.
